key_points: Raikes program students praised for having amazing dorms
Gender-inclusive housing options exist for LGBTQ+ students
Large campus size intimidates students coming from smaller environments
key_quotes: Amazing dorms [in the Raikes program]
I would second gender inclusive housing. Everyone I knew who went that route was queer of some sort and very accepting.

key_points: Described as classic big American college experience with parties and sports
Exchange students specifically seek out UNL for party culture
Big 10 school energy with large college-age student population around town
key_quotes: If you want the Animal House, huge campus experience, you'll want to go to places like Iowa State, Illinois, Nebraska
Big public universities in college towns like Kansas and Nebraska have a ton of college age students around

key_points: No direct student feedback on dining quality found in data
Campus food experience not mentioned in any posts or comments
Insufficient data to score meaningfully
key_quotes: No quotes available from this data set

key_points: Students with depression report fear of feeling lost in large student body
Finding learning communities recommended as key coping strategy at large schools
No dedicated mental health resources specifically mentioned for UNL
key_quotes: I have depression and used to be homeschooled, so I had gotten used to feeling like a nobody. I'm afraid that if I go to a big college, that feeling will just intensify
What helped me was finding a learning community at school, which is a group of students in a similar situation

key_points: Merit scholarships available and can be generous — one student got $13K/year
Out-of-state and international students get little to no need-based aid
Supplemental scholarship application opens in October — easy to miss
key_quotes: I got a letter stating I got a scholarship for $13,000 per year. IT MADE ME SO HAPPY
Public universities will rarely give financial aid to out-of-state students
Nebraska has a supplemental scholarship application that opens in October. Did they give you a merit scholarship with your acceptance?

key_points: Raikes program (CS + business honors cohort) highly regarded, seen as elite track
Meteorology/atmospheric science program is a genuine differentiator vs. peer schools
University of Nebraska Omaha doesn't offer tutoring for College Algebra — students sent to noisy Math Lab
key_quotes: Raikes is an honor to be selected for and I do believe the education I would receive there would be superior
UNL offers meteorology, and Iowa does not
My school doesn't offer tutoring for College Algebra students; those who need guidance should go to the Math Lab. I can't focus in the Math Lab

key_points: Scholarship notification process is unclear — students unsure how or when they'll hear
Admissions email response times are slow for international students
No major administrative horror stories surfaced, but processes lack transparency
key_quotes: I've gotten accepted to The University of Nebraska but they didn't say anything about scholarships
I sent an email to uno admissions about the same, but have not heard anything yet

key_points: Nebraska is geographically isolated — 'in the middle of nowhere transport wise'
Lincoln described as a classic college town with strong campus-centric culture
Weather and remoteness are consistent deterrents for out-of-state students
key_quotes: Nebraska is in the middle of nowhere transport wise
I wouldn't be too excited about UN Lincoln
UNL is really a great school... I definitely understand wanting to leave your current surroundings

key_points: Warren Buffett's undergraduate degree is from UNL — frequently cited as a credential boost
Raikes program positions students well for CS and business careers
Regional job market concentration limits national opportunities without grad school
key_quotes: Warren Buffett: University of Nebraska Lincoln, Master of Science: Columbia University
State schools typically have better recognition near their location, so if you choose to move after grad, get a masters elsewhere and you'll be fine
Warren Buffett is based for sending his kid to University of Nebraska

key_points: In-state tuition is genuinely affordable with strong merit aid stacking
Out-of-state students struggle to make the finances work without major scholarships
Raikes program at ~$9K OOS is considered exceptional value by students comparing it to $40K+ alternatives
key_quotes: As an OOS (but neighboring state) student, I would only be paying around 9k, which is financially the best option for my family
I applied to University of Nebraska-Lincoln... I got a scholarship for $13,000 per year
Public universities will rarely give financial aid to out-of-state students. It is very difficult to pull together enough scholarships to cover the full cost

key_points: Classic Midwestern big state school — football, Greek life, college town feel
Students who visit tend to like it; reputation suffers from coastal bias more than reality
Best fit for in-state students or those with specific programs like Raikes or meteorology
key_quotes: UNL is really a great school
I visited and liked it a lot even though it was super [cold/flat]
On this list, I wouldn't be too excited about UN Lincoln
